title: Guidance for Providing and Using Administrative Data for Statistical Purposes
scope: GIA use of federal administrative data for research and statistical analysis
description: Establishes standards for accessing, protecting, and using administrative data while maintaining privacy and enabling evidence-based decision making

principles.title: Data Access Transparency
principles.description: Agencies must document and disclose what administrative data is being accessed

principles.title: Statistical vs. Operational Use Distinction
principles.description: Administrative data used for statistics must be clearly separated from operational use

principles.title: Privacy Protection in Data Sharing
principles.description: De-identification standards must be applied to shared administrative data

principles.title: Data Governance Agreements
principles.description: Formal agreements required when sharing administrative data between agencies

principles.title: Audit and Accountability
principles.description: All administrative data access must be logged and auditable

controls.description: Access logging for all administrative data queries
Quarterly review of statistical data usage
Block operational use of statistical datasets

violationScenarios.violation: Using administrative data for non-statistical purposes
Sharing identifiable administrative data without agreement
